bullets: • SBI Small Cap Fund carries an exit load of 1.0%, meaning investors are charged this fee if they redeem their units before the stipulated holding period, as per data from https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-small-cap-fund-direct-plan-growth-3603
• SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund has an exit load of 0%, so no exit fee is charged upon redemption; however, it comes with a mandatory 3-year lock-in period during which redemption is not permitted at all.

• SBI Large Cap Fund (Bluechip) has a tiered exit load structure: 0.25% if redeemed within 0–30 days, and 0.1% if redeemed between 30–90 days, as per data from https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-bluechip-fund-direct-growth-3046
• Exit load is a fee levied when an investor exits or redeems their mutual fund units earlier than a stipulated period, and it is deducted from the redemption amount at the time of withdrawal.

• Among the three funds listed, SBI Small Cap Fund has the highest exit load at 1.0%, followed by SBI Large Cap Fund with a maximum of 0.25% (within 0–30 days), while SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund charges 0% exit load.

• The expense ratios for the direct plans are: SBI Small Cap Fund at 0.79%, SBI Large Cap Fund at 0.84%, and SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund at 0.99%; these are separate annual charges and are distinct from exit load fees.
